settings.heading: Managed services support for recurring business work
settings.text: Use managed services when recurring operational tasks, admin workflows or follow-through need a clearer support rhythm and agreed scope.

settings.heading: What managed support can include
settings.text: Managed services are scoped around practical recurring work, communication rhythm and clear ownership.

settings.title: Recurring support rhythm
settings.text: Agree the recurring tasks, review cadence, communication channel and expected handover points.

settings.title: Operational follow-through
settings.text: Support can cover admin workflows, registers, documentation upkeep, coordination and practical operating tasks where agreed.

settings.title: Status and escalation
settings.text: Define what needs status reporting, what should be escalated and what information the business owner needs to provide.

settings.title: What is not included
settings.text: Managed services do not include legal, tax, accounting, financial, insurance, HR, valuation or regulated professional advice unless separately arranged through a qualified provider.

settings.title: Boundaries and exclusions
settings.text: Emergency response, unlimited implementation, platform administration without access, specialist professional work and unscoped project delivery are excluded unless separately agreed.

settings.title: Request support
settings.text: Share the recurring work, current pain point, expected volume, tools involved, access requirements and timing.

settings.heading: Request managed services support
settings.text: Send the recurring work, business context, current systems, support frequency and any constraints so RBP can assess the right next step.
settings.aside_text: Include task types, frequency, tools, owners, current blockers, access requirements, deadlines and whether the work needs setup before recurring support starts.
